description: The agent-form tool catalog wire shape (Feature ③ P4). GET /available-tools on a mock workspace returns the built-in tool pool — each item carries id, description, source, and the JSON-Schema inputSchema. Pins the AvailableTool envelope (incl. the 0.5.0 serverId/serverLabel/inputSchema fields) cross-runtime; live remote-MCP tool enumeration needs a reachable server and stays in the runtime unit/integration tests.

items.description: List the knowledge bases available in this workspace, returning each one's `knowledgeBaseId`, name, and description. Use this when the user asks what data sources exist, or to discover which `knowledgeBaseId` to pass to `search_kb` before searching.

List documents (titles + ids) in a knowledge base. Pass `knowledgeBaseId` to scope to one KB; omit to list across every KB in the workspace. Useful when the user asks 'what's in my data' at the document level.

knowledgeBaseId.description: UUID of a knowledge base to scope to. Omit to list documents across every KB in the workspace.

items.description: Count how many documents are registered in a knowledge base. Pass `knowledgeBaseId` to scope; omit to count across every KB in the workspace. Cheaper than list_documents when only the magnitude matters.

knowledgeBaseId.description: UUID of a knowledge base. Omit to count workspace-wide.

items.description: Quick at-a-glance summary of a knowledge base: name, description, document count, and a short sample of document titles. Pass `knowledgeBaseId` to summarize one KB; omit to summarize each KB in the workspace. Use this for meta queries like 'what's in my data?' or 'tell me about my workspace'.

knowledgeBaseId.description: UUID of a knowledge base. Omit to summarize every KB in the workspace.

items.description: Semantic search across the workspace's knowledge bases. Returns the top matching chunks with a short content preview. Use this when the user asks a content question that requires looking inside the documents (not just listing them). Pass `knowledgeBaseId` to scope to one KB — call `list_kbs` first if you don't know which knowledge bases exist; omit `knowledgeBaseId` to search every KB.

knowledgeBaseId.description: UUID of a knowledge base to scope to. Omit to search workspace-wide.

limit.description: Max chunks to return (default 5, hard cap 8).

query.description: Natural-language question or phrase to search for, e.g. 'how does the billing flow work?'.

items.description: Fetch a single document's metadata + a short content preview. Use after list_documents/search_kb when the user asks for details on a specific item.

Return the literal chunks (rows / passages) of a specific document, in order. Use this when the user asks for actual content — e.g. 'show me 4 rows of the CSV', 'what's in the document', 'first few entries' — rather than a semantic question that would route through search_kb. Pass `limit` and `offset` to page; chunks are returned sorted by `chunkIndex`.

limit.description: Max chunks to return (default 10, hard cap 50).

offset.description: Number of leading chunks to skip — use to page beyond the first batch.
